prompt: Analyze and refactor a large CI/CD pipeline configuration. The pipeline configuration is at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/ci/pipeline.yml' - a 500+ line YAML file defining a complex multi-stage deployment pipeline. There are also environment-specific override files: ci/pipeline-prod.yml, ci/pipeline-staging.yml, and ci/pipeline-dev.yml (each 100-200 lines). Additionally, there are reusable workflow definitions in ci/workflows/ directory: build.yml, test.yml, deploy.yml, notify.yml, and security-scan.yml (each 50-150 lines). Your task: 1) Read the main pipeline.yml and all override files completely. 2) Read all workflow definition files. 3) Analyze the pipeline for: duplicate step definitions across environments, hardcoded values that should be variables or secrets, missing error handling (no timeout, no retry logic), stages that could run in parallel but are sequential, inconsistent naming conventions, steps that run even when previous steps fail (missing condition checks). 4) Identify optimization opportunities: caching opportunities (package managers, build artifacts), parallel execution groups, reusable components that could be extracted into shared workflows, steps that can be skipped based on file changes (path-based triggers). 5) Generate an optimized version of the main pipeline at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/ci/pipeline-optimized.yml' that: uses variables/templates to eliminate duplication, implements proper caching, parallelizes independent stages, adds timeout and retry logic, uses consistent naming, includes proper condition checks. 6) Create an optimization report at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/pipeline-optimization.txt' documenting: current vs optimized pipeline metrics (estimated minutes saved), list of all changes made with justification, before/after comparisons for key sections, and a migration checklist for adopting the new pipeline. CRITICAL: This deploys to a Kubernetes cluster handling 1M+ RPS. You must also: 1) Implement canary analysis with automated promotion. 2) Add chaos engineering tests. 3) Create disaster recovery procedures. 4) Generate SLO/SLA dashboards. 5) Implement cost optimization strategies.
